Stories

  • The Summer That Refused to End (forthcoming)

    khōréō

  • How to Come Back to Yourself (forthcoming)

    The Tarot of Love, Flame Tree Press

  • Ludonarrative Dissonance (forthcoming)

    Lightspeed

  • (Non)deterministic Finite Automata

    Fusion Fragment, July 2026

    The universe where Chen and Ranyi were born, research progressed enough to identify convergence points, where the same threads aligned. The game became: here are versions of the same situation. Here are the choices you can make and all the new possibilities that spawn off from each split, each divide. Find the one where you win.

    Everyone should get to write a multiverse story! This one is mine (also features state machines, soulmates, and space)

  • Underneath the Underneath

    Strange Horizons, April 2026

    The thing is that Mina wouldn’t mind being a mother.

    She thinks about it when she babysits. She imagines the swell of her stomach, as tender and slight as the moon, and the motion inside of her like the tides, rocking, of the baby. She imagines how simple love could be, how intrinsic, how it might feel to hold someone the way her mother held her. She could do it and it would be beautiful, and if she had a son he could hunt the dragons and if she had a daughter Mina could teach her how to tie knots.

    But not minding and wanting are different things.

    Reproductive freedom, womanhood, and dragons.

  • Six Sides of a Fairy Tale

    Lightspeed, February 2026

    Sometimes, a prophecy was just another kind of story: If you told them like they were the truth, that could be enough to believe in them.

    A prophet, her princess, and six ways of telling it.

    • Recommended by Maria Haskins in Locus #783, reviewed as "a cleverly constructed fairytale retelling" that muses on "the difference between telling the truth and telling a good story."
  • Mother's Mother's Daughter

    Silk and Sinew: A Collection of Folk Horror From the Asian Diaspora, May 2025

    All witches are their mother’s daughters, made of magic. And as all magic is, it was a contract: my mother planted the flesh and bone, hewn neatly at the ankle, in the garden. In return for her sacrifice, I sprouted after a year.

    Concretizing the sacrifices immigrant parents and eldest daughters make with contracts, a garden, and some body horror. Also, Shirley Jackson Award-nominated for Short Fiction!

    • Anthology available for purchase here. Silk and Sinew has also been named one of NYPL's top horror books of 2025, won the Bram Stoker and is nominated for the British Fantasy Award and the Shirley Jackson Award for best anthology.
    • Selected for The Best Weird Fiction of the Year Vol 2, available for preorder here.
    • Hear from some contributors!
    • I talk more about the inspiration behind th story here
  • Two's Company, Three Might Be a Sign of Demonic Posssession

    PseudoPod, March 2025

    You didn’t take the usual precautions when Lin died.

    Mostly what it says on the tin (necromancy, demonic posession, etc etc).

    • Recipient of 2025 Brave New Weird Award (to be reprinted in Brave New Weird Vol. 4)!
    • Recommended on the 2025 Nebula Suggested Reading List.
    • Recommended by Angela Liu here!
  • Five Stages of Shedding a Skin

    Orion's Belt, July 2024

    The only reason anyone gets a new skin is to become someone else.

    The five stages of...grief?

    • Interviewed on Orion Belt's Patreon.
  • Threshold

    Strange Horizons, March 2024

    When Granny came by Li’s workshop to deliver the news of Huyuan’s death, the choice in front of Li was hardly a choice at all, and even Granny knew it.

    “Make sure you’re building a body and not a tomb,” Granny said.

    My first sale! Ft. body-building of a literal nature, girls in love (?), and the usual warnings about messing with death.

    • Recommended in Strange Horizon's 2025 Hugo Awards Primer.
    • In Gautam Bhatia's newsletter!
    • Interviewed by UNC-Ch's Department of English and Comparative Literature.
